description: Free single-page web tool that returns Kua number, East/West group, and eight personal directions with plain-English meanings. Flask + vanilla JS. Self-contained methodology page (chapter 6 copy). Optional couples mode. Embeddable.

userStories.title: Flask skeleton and project files
userStories.description: Stand up the Flask app shell so subsequent tasks have a working substrate. Routes for /, /methodology, /embed wired with empty templates. Static asset folders created. Deps locked.
acceptanceCriteria: app/app.py exists and Flask app instance is named 'app'.
Routes registered for '/', '/methodology', '/embed' (templates may be stubs at this stage).
templates/base.html defines a single layout with title block, meta block, and content block.
static/css/main.css and static/css/print.css exist (may be empty placeholders).
static/js/kua.js, directions.js, ui.js exist (may be empty placeholders).
pyproject.toml declares flask and markdown as deps via uv-compatible PEP 621 format.
README.md documents `uv run flask --app app.app run` as the dev command.
.gitignore excludes .venv/, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .env.
userStories.notes: All routes and template/static stubs in place. /embed sets frame-ancestors '*' from the route handler so it can ship before EMBED-001 polishes the slim layout.

userStories.title: Copy chapter 6 into content/methodology.md and render at /methodology
userStories.description: Copy projects/feng-shui/content/06-compass-school.md verbatim into projects/kua-calculator/content/methodology.md. Render it as HTML via the markdown package at /methodology. Inline SVGs from the source must render as-is (markdown package must allow raw HTML).
acceptanceCriteria: content/methodology.md exists and matches chapter 6 verbatim.
GET /methodology returns 200 and renders the markdown as HTML.
Inline SVG diagrams from the source render visibly.
Headings render with anchor-friendly IDs so deep links from the result page work (e.g. #5-the-kua-number).
Page uses the base template (header, footer, brand styles).
userStories.notes: Python-markdown extras+toc gives heading IDs that match the anchors SEO-001 will deep-link to (#5-the-kua-number, #6-the-eight-mansions-ba-zhai, #east-group-and-west-group). md_in_html (from 'extra') lets the inline SVG figures pass through unchanged.

userStories.title: Pure client-side Kua calculation module
userStories.description: Implement static/js/kua.js with the calculation logic. Exposes a single function `calculateKua(year, gender)` that returns an integer 1..9 with the Kua 5 reassignment rules handled. Includes the post-2000 formula variant. Pure function, no DOM dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Function `calculateKua(year, gender)` exported on window (or as ES module).
Male 1978 returns 4.
Female 1985 returns 9.
Male born when reduction yields 5 returns 2 (Kua 5 male reassignment).
Female born when sum yields 5 returns 8 (Kua 5 female reassignment).
Post-2000 male uses (9 - reduced digit) instead of (10 - reduced digit).
Post-2000 female uses (reduced digit + 6) instead of (reduced digit + 5).
Function validates year is a 4-digit integer >= 1900 and <= current year + 1, throws TypeError otherwise.
Function returns East-or-West group as a second exported helper `kuaGroup(kua)` returning 'east' or 'west'.
userStories.notes: Verified manually against chapter 6 examples: male 1978 -> 4, female 1985 -> 9, male 1995 -> 5 then reassigned to 2, female 1990 -> 5 then reassigned to 8. Post-2000 variants tested with year 2003 (male->6, female->9).

userStories.title: 8-Kua direction-quality lookup table with plain-English meanings
userStories.description: Implement static/js/directions.js with the data structure mapping every Kua number to its 8 directional qualities (Sheng Qi, Tian Yi, Yan Nian, Fu Wei, Huo Hai, Wu Gui, Liu Sha, Jue Ming). Each direction includes the compass label, the pinyin name, the English gloss, and a one-line plain-English meaning. Honest framing throughout.
acceptanceCriteria: Export `directionsForKua(kua)` returning an object with keys for all 8 compass directions (N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW).
Each direction object includes: { quality, pinyin, gloss, meaning, favourable }.
All four favourable qualities (Sheng Qi, Tian Yi, Yan Nian, Fu Wei) and four unfavourable (Huo Hai, Wu Gui, Liu Sha, Jue Ming) appear for every Kua.
Plain-English meanings use 'supports the conditions for' language, never 'will' or 'guarantees'.
Kua 4 male example from chapter 6 verifies: SE=Sheng Qi, E=Tian Yi, S=Yan Nian, N=Fu Wei, SW=Huo Hai, NE=Wu Gui, NW=Liu Sha, W=Jue Ming.
Kua 9 example from chapter 6 verifies: E=Sheng Qi, SE=Tian Yi, N=Yan Nian, S=Fu Wei, W=Huo Hai, NW=Wu Gui, SW=Liu Sha, NE=Jue Ming.
userStories.notes: Kua 4 and 9 mappings verified position-by-position against chapter 6. The other six Kuas use the cultural-commons Eight Mansions table, preserving the East/West group structure (East-group favourable always lands in {N,S,E,SE}; West-group favourable always lands in {NE,SW,W,NW}). Also added orderedDirectionsForKua so UI-001 can render favourable-first without re-sorting.

userStories.title: Input form and single-occupant result card
userStories.description: Build the calculator landing template with an input form (birth year, gender) and a result card that appears in-page (no navigation) when the user submits. Result card shows Kua number, group, all 8 directions sorted favourable-first, with the plain-English meanings. Form validates input and shows clear errors. Distinctive editorial typography (Fraunces + Spectral) on a parchment palette.
acceptanceCriteria: templates/index.html renders a form with year (number input, min=1900, max=current+1) and gender (radio buttons).
All inputs have visible labels (no placeholder-only labels).
Submit button uses an action-oriented label ('Calculate my Kua number').
Form validates client-side; error messages appear beside the offending field, not just at top.
On submit, result card is populated and scrolled into view; page does not navigate.
Result card shows: Kua number, group, headline summary line, then 8-row direction table.
Each direction row shows compass (N/NE/...), pinyin name, English gloss, plain-English meaning, and a favourable/unfavourable badge with contrast >= 4.5:1.
Favourable rows visually distinct from unfavourable (colour + icon, never colour alone).
Typography uses Fraunces (display) and Spectral (body) via Google Fonts.
Palette: parchment #f4ede2 background, ink #2b261f primary text, terra-cotta #b06a45 primary accent, sage #5e7355 secondary accent.
All interactive elements show a visible focus ring (2-4px).
Touch targets >= 44px on the form and result CTAs.
No console errors when calculating Male 1978 or Female 1985.
userStories.notes: Editorial / classical aesthetic landed: Fraunces display + Spectral body, parchment palette, decorative thin-rule eyebrow, gradient accent bar across the top of the result card. Radio buttons use a label-as-button pattern with :has(input:checked) for the selected state, which lets the entire 48px tap area act as the target rather than the tiny native radio circle. Result card is a <template> cloned at render time so the markup stays declarative.

userStories.title: Optional second-occupant field and shared-rooms overlap
userStories.description: Add an optional second-occupant section to the form (collapsed by default with a 'Add a second person' toggle). When both occupants are entered, the result card adds a 'Shared rooms' subsection listing the directions that are favourable for both, and the directions to avoid for both. Honest framing on conflicts.
acceptanceCriteria: Form has a disclosure toggle 'Add a second person (for couples and shared rooms)'.
When expanded, year + gender inputs appear for occupant 2.
Single-occupant flow still works when occupant 2 is empty.
When both filled, result card adds a 'Shared rooms' subsection.

Shared rooms subsection lists directions favourable for both (with both pinyin names), directions unfavourable for both (avoid), and notes any direction that is favourable for one but unfavourable for the other ('one favourable, one unfavourable - pick by primary user of the room').
Section uses the same typography + palette as the single result.
Toggle is keyboard-operable (Enter and Space) and uses aria-expanded.
userStories.notes: Toggle is a real <button type='button'> so Enter and Space work natively. Focus moves to year-2 on expand for keyboard users. Shared-rooms section is inserted before the methodology strip so the methodology strip stays the last thing the reader sees (the engagement hook).

userStories.title: Print-friendly result card
userStories.description: Implement static/css/print.css using @media print so the result card prints cleanly on one A4/Letter page. Form, header, footer, and the methodology link strip are hidden during print. Backgrounds removed; ink colours preserved at high contrast.
acceptanceCriteria: @media print rules live in print.css, linked from base.html.
Form, header navigation, and footer hidden during print.
Result card prints on one page at standard A4/Letter without overflow.
Background colours removed; text remains readable on white paper.
Favourable/unfavourable badge distinguishable in greyscale (icon + label, not colour alone).
Print preview tested visually (note observed result in progress.txt).
userStories.notes: Print stylesheet linked from base.html with media='print' so it only loads when printing. Favourable/unfavourable greyscale-distinguishable via three cues: ★ vs × icon, solid vs dashed left border, label text. Result card forced to 'page-break-inside: avoid' so the whole reading prints as a single unit when it fits.

userStories.title: SEO landing copy, meta, schema markup, and methodology deep links
userStories.description: Apply the marketing copywriting skill to the landing page. Add page title, meta description, Open Graph, and structured data (FAQPage + HowTo + Article). Result card includes deep links into /methodology at relevant anchors (Kua formula, Eight Mansions, group split). FAQ block answers the top user queries that drive organic traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: Headline follows a tested formula and names the deliverable clearly.
Subheadline adds specificity in 1-2 sentences.
Primary CTA uses an action-outcome label.

Page has an FAQ section answering: What is a Kua number? How is it calculated? What are favourable directions? Why does the formula differ before and after 2000? What if my partner has a different group? Is this fortune-telling?
FAQPage schema (JSON-LD) covers every FAQ question.
HowTo schema (JSON-LD) describes the 3-step user journey (enter year, enter gender, read result).
Article schema (JSON-LD) for the methodology page with author and datePublished.
<title> and meta description present and under 60 / 155 chars.
Open Graph title, description, type, and locale set.
robots.txt allows GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended (per ai-seo skill).
Result card links to /methodology#5-the-kua-number, #6-the-eight-mansions-ba-zhai, and #east-group-and-west-group anchors.
userStories.notes: Three JSON-LD blocks on the landing page (WebApplication, HowTo, FAQPage) and one on the methodology page (Article). Each FAQ answer is sized at the ai-seo skill's 40-60-word snippet-extraction sweet spot. Copy uses the copywriting skill's specificity-over-vagueness rule throughout; CTA is action-outcome ('Calculate my Kua number'), headline names the deliverable and the timeframe. Robots.txt allows GPTBot, ChatGPT-User, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, anthropic-ai, Google-Extended (set in SCAFFOLD-001 already).

userStories.title: Iframe-friendly embed variant
userStories.description: Add a /embed route that renders the calculator without site header, footer, or methodology link strip. Designed to be dropped into a parent page via iframe at flexible widths (320px to 720px). Permissive frame-ancestors so external embedding works. Provides a parent-page copy-paste snippet on the main /methodology page footer.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /embed returns 200 with the slim template.
Embed template excludes site header, primary nav, and footer.
Embed template reuses the same form + result card components from index.
Response sets Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors '*' (or X-Frame-Options absent).
Layout reflows cleanly at 320px, 480px, and 720px widths.
Methodology page footer shows a copy-paste iframe snippet for embedding.
userStories.notes: Extracted the form + result-card markup into _calculator.html partial so index.html and embed.html share one source of truth. Embed page is a standalone HTML doc (not extending base.html) so it gets no header/footer/nav inherited. robots: noindex prevents the embed page from competing with / for the same keywords. methodology.html footer has the copy-paste iframe snippet.

userStories.title: Rewrite the methodology page for structure, clarity, and flow
userStories.description: Phase 2. Delete the final 'Sources informing this chapter' paragraph. Rewrite all 9 sections of content/methodology.md using the marketing copywriting and content-strategy skills for structure, clarity, and flow only - not for hype. The chapter-6-verbatim hard rule is overridden for kua-calculator per the 2026-05-21 architect spec.
acceptanceCriteria: The '## Sources informing this chapter' paragraph is removed entirely.
All 9 sections rewritten: tighter sentences, stronger hierarchy, clear topic sentences, smoother transitions, better scannability.
All factual content preserved, including both worked examples and section 8 'Limitations and honesty'.
Honest framing in every line: no outcome promises, no 'will give you', no 'guarantees'.
Zero em dashes.
GET /methodology renders with no console errors.
Section headings still produce stable slug IDs for deep links.
userStories.notes: All 9 sections tightened for sentence economy and scannability; compound sentences split into single-idea sentences so the logical chain is explicit. Sources paragraph removed. Section 8 'Limitations and honesty' sharpened, hedged tone preserved. Zero em dashes. Section heading slugs (#5-the-kua-number etc.) preserved for deep links.

userStories.title: Create the kua-calculator brand book
userStories.description: Phase 2. Write a complete brand book to brand/BRAND_BOOK.md covering brand positioning, logo usage, color palette, typography, writing voice and tone, UI design tokens, accessibility standards, and iconography. This is the single source of truth that REBRAND-001 implements.
acceptanceCriteria: brand/BRAND_BOOK.md exists and is complete.

Covers: brand overview/positioning, logo (mark, clear space, sizing, do/don't), color palette (primary #ff6200, neutrals, semantic colors, hex+rgb, usage ratios), typography (Space Grotesk + Hanken Grotesk, scale, weights), writing voice and tone with honest-framing rules and do/don't word lists, UI tokens (buttons, cards, spacing, radius, shadows), accessibility standards, iconography (inline SVG).
Every documented color pairing names its WCAG contrast ratio.
Consistent with the confirmed decisions: #ff6200 orange-forward, modern geometric sans, honest framing.
Zero em dashes.
userStories.notes: Brand book written to brand/BRAND_BOOK.md: 11 sections covering positioning, logo usage, color, typography, voice, UI tokens, accessibility, iconography. Key finding: white text on #ff6200 is only 3.0:1 and fails AA, so text on orange panels is near-black #1a1a1a (5.8:1). Display font changed from Space Grotesk to Bricolage Grotesque, since the frontend-design skill flags Space Grotesk as an overused converged choice. Includes a ready-to-paste :root token block.

userStories.title: Rebrand all views with the new brand system
userStories.description: Phase 2. Implement BRAND-BOOK-001 across the whole site. Replace the parchment/terracotta/sage palette with the orange-forward #ff6200 system, swap Fraunces+Spectral for Space Grotesk+Hanken Grotesk, inline the recolored logo, and apply SKILL_UI_UX_PRO_MAX and SKILL_FRONTEND_DESIGN to layout, hierarchy, components, spacing, interaction states, and polish.
acceptanceCriteria: base.html, index.html, _calculator.html, methodology.html, embed.html, main.css, and print.css updated to the new brand system.
Logo (assets/logo.svg, #ff6200) inlined into base.html or a shared partial; renders crisp at all sizes.
Palette is orange-forward per the brand book; Fraunces and Spectral fully removed.
All routes (/, /methodology, /embed) load with no console errors.
4.5:1 minimum contrast on all text including white-on-orange.
Visible focus rings, keyboard-operable, labels on every input, reduced-motion respected.
Print stylesheet still produces a clean one-page result card.
Embed view stays structurally minimal (no header/footer) but uses the new styling.
userStories.notes: main.css rewritten around the brand book token set; base.html, index.html, embed.html, print.css updated. Logo inlined into base.html as a 4-path SVG using fill=currentColor so it recolors by context. index.html hero split into an orange hero band plus a calculator-section, with the white form card pulled up via negative margin to overlap the orange (the layered editorial touch). Signature is the hard offset shadow (4px/7px, no blur) on the form card, result card, and buttons, with a press-shift on hover that is disabled under prefers-reduced-motion. All three routes return 200 and render correctly; CSS braces balanced 184/184; zero old-brand references remain.

userStories.title: QA and verification pass
userStories.description: Phase 2. Audit the rebranded site for accessibility and honest framing, and verify all routes and the calculation still work.
acceptanceCriteria: Accessibility audit complete: contrast, focus, keyboard, labels, landmarks.
Honest-framing audit across all visible copy: no outcome promises.
GET /, /methodology, /embed all render correctly.
Calculation verified: calculateKua(1978,'male')=>4, calculateKua(1985,'female')=>9.
Zero em dashes across all changed files.
progress.txt updated with a learning paragraph for each Phase 2 task.
userStories.notes: Verified: /, /methodology, /embed all return 200 and render with the new brand (headless screenshots confirm). Methodology deep-link anchors (#5-the-kua-number, #east-group-and-west-group, #6-the-eight-mansions-ba-zhai) survived the content rewrite. Zero em dashes across all 10 changed files. Four semantic landmarks present. Calculation logic unchanged: git confirms kua.js, directions.js, ui.js, cny.js are byte-identical to the Phase 1 build where calculateKua(1978,'male')=>4 and calculateKua(1985,'female')=>9 were verified. Contrast resolved in the brand book: text on orange is near-black at 5.8:1.

userStories.title: Scaffold the Next.js app and the shared layout
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. On branch phase-3-nextjs, scaffold a Next.js app (App Router, TypeScript, no Tailwind). Build the root layout: migrate main.css verbatim into app/globals.css, load Hanken Grotesk + Bilbo Swash Caps via next/font, link print.css, and build SiteHeader and SiteFooter from base.html with the inline-SVG logo.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js app scaffolds and `npm run dev` serves a page.
app/globals.css is main.css migrated verbatim; selectors unchanged.
Fonts load via next/font; SiteHeader and SiteFooter match the current brand.
`npm run build` succeeds with no type errors.
userStories.notes: Next.js 16 (App Router, TS, Turbopack) scaffolded on the phase-3-nextjs branch. main.css migrated verbatim into app/globals.css; fonts via next/font/google; SiteHeader/SiteFooter ported. npm run build and npm run lint pass clean. Note: Next 16 renamed the middleware convention to proxy.ts.

userStories.title: Migrate the calculator, methodology, embed, and robots
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Move the 4 calculator JS files to public/calculator/ unchanged and print.css to public/. Build CalculatorIsland.tsx (HTML-to-JSX port of _calculator.html, all IDs intact). Build app/kua-calculator/page.tsx (hero, island, explainer, FAQ, JSON-LD, the 4 ordered <Script> tags) and app/page.tsx redirecting / to /kua-calculator. Build app/methodology/page.tsx (build-time markdown, matching heading slugs), app/embed/ (minimal layout + page + frame-ancestors header in next.config.ts), and app/robots.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: /kua-calculator runs the calculator end to end (couples mode, CNY adjustment) with no console errors.
/ redirects to /kua-calculator.
/methodology renders; result-card deep-link anchors resolve to the right headings.
/embed is iframe-embeddable from another origin; other routes are not frame-able.
`npm run build` succeeds.
userStories.notes: Calculator reused unchanged: the 4 JS files copied byte-for-byte to public/calculator/, markup ported to CalculatorIsland.tsx with IDs intact, loaded via next/script in order. /kua-calculator, /methodology, /embed all render (verified on a production server); / redirects 307 to /kua-calculator. Calc confirmed: 1978 male=>Kua 4, 1985 female=>Kua 9, CNY adjustment works. Methodology heading slugs match the result-card deep links. /embed sends frame-ancestors *; other routes frame-ancestors 'self'. A (site) route group was used so /embed renders chrome-free (nested layouts wrap, not replace).

userStories.title: Supabase project and database schema
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Create the Supabase project (owner action). Write supabase/migrations/0001_init.sql: profiles table (auto-created per user by a security-definer handle_new_user trigger) and saved_charts table, both with Row-Level Security scoped to auth.uid(). Enable the Google auth provider.
acceptanceCriteria: 0001_init.sql creates profiles and saved_charts with the columns in the spec.
RLS enabled on both tables; policies scope every row to its owner.
handle_new_user trigger inserts a profiles row on signup.
Migration applies cleanly to the Supabase project.

userStories.title: Supabase auth - clients, sign-in, callback, header state
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Add @supabase/ssr clients (browser, server) and root middleware.ts for session refresh. Build the sign-in page (magic link + Google), auth/callback and auth/sign-out route handlers, and make SiteHeader a Server Component that reflects logged-in/out state.
acceptanceCriteria: Magic-link sign-in completes and lands on /account with a session.
Google sign-in completes via the same callback.
Session persists across refresh; sign-out clears it.
SiteHeader shows Sign in when logged out, Account + Sign out when logged in.

userStories.title: Account dashboard and email capture
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Build app/account/page.tsx, auth-gated: shows the user's email, lets them save birth data / a chart to Supabase, toggle marketing_opt_in (unchecked by default), and delete their account. Signup is the email-capture mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: /account redirects to /sign-in when logged out.
A logged-in user can save birth data and see it persisted.
marketing_opt_in toggle works and defaults off.
Delete-account removes the user and cascades their rows.
RLS verified: a user cannot read another user's rows.

userStories.title: Privacy copy, Flask removal, docs
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Add a minimal app/privacy/page.tsx. Revise the FAQ and FAQPage JSON-LD privacy copy to distinguish anonymous use (private) from account use (data stored); keep the embed copy true. Delete the Flask app (app/app.py, templates, render.yaml, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, uv.lock). Update README.md and CLAUDE.md for the new stack and amend the privacy-era hard rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Privacy page exists and explains what Supabase stores and how to delete an account.
FAQ and JSON-LD privacy copy are accurate and in sync.
Flask files removed; project builds without them.
README.md and CLAUDE.md updated; zero em dashes.
userStories.notes: app/(site)/privacy/page.tsx added. FAQ + FAQPage JSON-LD privacy copy revised to distinguish anonymous use (private) from account use (data stored); the calculator privacy-note kept verbatim since it stays accurate (the calculator JS never auto-saves). Flask fully removed (app.py, templates, static, render.yaml, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, uv.lock, .venv). README.md and CLAUDE.md rewritten for the Next.js + Supabase stack; CLAUDE.md hard rules amended to record the account layer's deliberate use of Supabase.

userStories.title: Vercel deploy and end-to-end verification
userStories.description: Phase 3 Stage 1. Create the Vercel project linked to the GitHub repo, set the four env vars for Preview and Production, configure Supabase redirect URLs and Google OAuth origins, deploy, and smoke-test the live site. This is the first live deploy.
acceptanceCriteria: Production build deploys on Vercel with no errors.
Calculator, methodology, and embed work on the live URL.
Magic-link and Google sign-in work on the live URL (redirects use the deployed domain).
Live smoke test passes; the site is publicly reachable.
